title: cynoglossum longipedicellatum
intro: Cynoglossum longipedicellatum, also known as the long-stalked hound's-tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to the mountainous regions of western China. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Cynoglossum longipedicellatum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate coughs and sore throats. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are taken to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
